9	// CLIOverrides carries per-invocation flag values that override
10	// project config. Zero values mean "no override; use config".
11	// Pointer fields distinguish "explicit value" from "not set".
12	type CLIOverrides struct {
13		Harness       string
14		Model         string
15		Provider      string
16		ModelRef      string
17		Profile       string
18		Effort        string
19		Permissions   string
20		MinTier       string
21		MaxTier       string
22		Timeout       *time.Duration
23		NoReview      *bool
24		Assignee      string
25		ContextBudget string
26		// ProviderRequestTimeout, when non-nil, overrides the per-request wall-clock
27		// cap applied to a single Chat/ChatStream call. Corresponds to --request-timeout
28		// on execute-bead and execute-loop. Zero pointer means "use config or model default".
29		ProviderRequestTimeout *time.Duration
30		// MinPower and MaxPower are passthrough power-bounds for the upstream agent
31		// routing contract (CONTRACT-003 / FEAT-006). DDx passes these to
32		// ServiceExecuteRequest unchanged; the agent owns model selection within the
33		// bounds. Zero means no bound (unconstrained). Corresponds to --min-power and
34		// --max-power on execute-bead and execute-loop.
35		MinPower int
36		MaxPower int
37		// OpaquePassthrough, when true, prevents Resolve from falling back to
38		// agent.harness / agent.model from the project config when the caller did
39		// not supply those values explicitly. Used by the ddx work path
40		// (FEAT-010 / ddx-c4231775): routing belongs to the agent service;
41		// DDx must not inject a config-sourced model or harness.
42		OpaquePassthrough bool
43	}

45	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto
46	// cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per field. The
47	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
48	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
49	// captured.
50	//
51	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
52	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
53	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
54		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
55	
56		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee
57		r.reviewMaxRetries = c.ResolveReviewMaxRetries()
58	
59		var agent *AgentConfig
60		var workers *WorkersConfig
61		if c != nil {
62			agent = c.Agent.Clone()
63			workers = c.Workers
64		}
65	
66		r.noProgressCooldown = workers.ResolveNoProgressCooldown()
67		r.maxNoChangesBeforeClose = workers.ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
68		r.heartbeatInterval = workers.ResolveHeartbeatInterval()
69	
70		r.harness = overrides.Harness
71		if r.harness == "" && !overrides.OpaquePassthrough && agent != nil {
72			r.harness = agent.Harness
73		}
74	
75		r.model = overrides.Model
76		if r.model == "" && !overrides.OpaquePassthrough && agent != nil {
77			r.model = agent.Model
78		}
79	
80		r.provider = overrides.Provider
81		r.modelRef = overrides.ModelRef
82		r.profile = overrides.Profile
83		r.minTier = overrides.MinTier
84		r.maxTier = overrides.MaxTier
85		r.effort = overrides.Effort
86		r.minPower = overrides.MinPower
87		r.maxPower = overrides.MaxPower
88		r.passthrough = AgentPassthrough{
89			Harness:  r.harness,
90			Provider: r.provider,
91			Model:    r.model,
92		}
93	
94		r.permissions = overrides.Permissions
95		if r.permissions == "" && agent != nil {
96			r.permissions = agent.Permissions
97		}
98	
99		if overrides.Timeout != nil {
100			r.timeout = *overrides.Timeout
101		} else if agent != nil && agent.TimeoutMS > 0 {
102			r.timeout = time.Duration(agent.TimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
103		}
104	
105		if overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout != nil {
106			r.providerRequestTimeout = *overrides.ProviderRequestTimeout
107		}
108	
109		r.evidenceCaps = c.ResolveEvidenceCaps(r.harness)
110	
111		r.contextBudget = overrides.ContextBudget
112		if r.contextBudget == "" && c != nil {
113			r.contextBudget = c.EvidenceCaps.ResolveContextBudget()
114		}
115	
116		if agent != nil {
117			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
118			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
119				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
120				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {
121					r.reasoningLevels[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)
122				}
123			}
124		}
125		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Mirror != nil {
126			r.mirrorConfig = c.Executions.Mirror.Clone()
127		}
128	
129		return r
130	}

132	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
133	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
134	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
135	// ResolvedConfig.
136	//
137	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed
138	// ResolvedConfig populated from package defaults plus the supplied
139	// overrides, alongside the underlying error. Callers decide whether to
140	// surface the error or proceed with the defaults-backed config.
141	//
142	// If projectRoot is empty, the process working directory is used.
143	func LoadAndResolve(projectRoot string, overrides CLIOverrides) (ResolvedConfig, error) {
144		cfg, err := LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
145		if err != nil {
146			return DefaultNewConfig().Resolve(overrides), err
147		}
148		return cfg.Resolve(overrides), nil
149	}

151	// ResolvedConfig is the loop/runner/reviewer's view of merged project
152	// config plus per-invocation overrides. It is constructed only by
153	// (*Config).Resolve / config.LoadAndResolve and is safe to share across
154	// goroutines (no method mutates it).
155	//
156	// Sealed-construction: every public accessor calls requireSealed on
157	// entry. A zero-value ResolvedConfig{} or `var r ResolvedConfig` is a
158	// valid Go expression but fails loudly on first read with a panic
159	// naming LoadAndResolve. See SD-024 / TD-024.
160	type ResolvedConfig struct {
161		sealed bool
162	
163		assignee                string
164		reviewMaxRetries        int
165		noProgressCooldown      time.Duration
166		maxNoChangesBeforeClose int
167		heartbeatInterval       time.Duration
168		harness                 string
169		model                   string
170		provider                string
171		modelRef                string
172		profile                 string
173		minTier                 string
174		maxTier                 string
175		effort                  string
176		minPower                int
177		maxPower                int
178		passthrough             AgentPassthrough
179		permissions             string
180		timeout                 time.Duration
181		wallClock               time.Duration
182		contextBudget           string
183		evidenceCaps            evidence.Caps
184		sessionLogDir           string
185		mirrorConfig            *ExecutionsMirrorConfig
186		reasoningLevels         map[string][]string
187		providerRequestTimeout  time.Duration
188	}
189	
190	// requireSealed panics if r was not produced by Resolve / LoadAndResolve.
191	// Called as the first statement of every public accessor.
192	func (r ResolvedConfig) requireSealed() {
193		if !r.sealed {
194			panic("config: ResolvedConfig used without going through " +
195				"(*Config).Resolve or config.LoadAndResolve. " +
196				"Production callers must obtain a ResolvedConfig from " +
197				"LoadAndResolve; tests must use NewTestConfigFor*.")
198		}
199	}

334	func cloneStringSliceMap(m map[string][]string) map[string][]string {
335		if m == nil {
336			return nil
337		}
338		out := make(map[string][]string, len(m))
339		for k, v := range m {
340			out[k] = append([]string(nil), v...)

5	// NewConfig represents the simplified DDx configuration structure
6	// This aligns with the schema defined in ADR-005 and SD-003
7	type NewConfig struct {
8		Version         string              `yaml:"version" json:"version"`
9		Library         *LibraryConfig      `yaml:"library" json:"library"`
10		Bead            *BeadConfig         `yaml:"bead,omitempty" json:"bead,omitempty"`
11		System          *SystemConfig       `yaml:"system,omitempty" json:"system,omitempty"`
12		PersonaBindings map[string]string   `yaml:"persona_bindings,omitempty" json:"persona_bindings,omitempty"`
13		UpdateCheck     *UpdateCheckConfig  `yaml:"update_check,omitempty" json:"update_check,omitempty"`
14		Agent           *AgentConfig        `yaml:"agent,omitempty" json:"agent,omitempty"`
15		Git             *GitConfig          `yaml:"git,omitempty" json:"git,omitempty"`
16		Server          *ServerConfig       `yaml:"server,omitempty" json:"server,omitempty"`
17		Executions      *ExecutionsConfig   `yaml:"executions,omitempty" json:"executions,omitempty"`
18		Cost            *CostConfig         `yaml:"cost,omitempty" json:"cost,omitempty"`
19		Workers         *WorkersConfig      `yaml:"workers,omitempty" json:"workers,omitempty"`
20		EvidenceCaps    *EvidenceCapsConfig `yaml:"evidence_caps,omitempty" json:"evidence_caps,omitempty"`
21		// ReviewMaxRetries caps reviewer attempts per committed result_rev before
22		// the execute-loop emits a terminal `review-manual-required` event and
23		// parks the bead (FEAT-022 §14). Zero or unset uses the binary default
24		// (3). Negative values are treated as unset.
25		ReviewMaxRetries *int `yaml:"review_max_retries,omitempty" json:"review_max_retries,omitempty"`
26	}
27	
28	// ResolveReviewMaxRetries returns the effective reviewer retry cap for this
29	// config. Defaults to 3 when unset (matching agent.DefaultReviewMaxRetries).
30	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveReviewMaxRetries() int {
31		if c == nil || c.ReviewMaxRetries == nil || *c.ReviewMaxRetries <= 0 {
32			return 3
33		}
34		return *c.ReviewMaxRetries
35	}
36	
37	// EvidenceCapsConfig configures byte-size caps used by the shared
38	// evidence-assembly primitives (FEAT-022 §1a). Project-level fields
39	// override the binary defaults; entries in `per_harness` further
40	// override the project-level values for a specific harness name.
41	type EvidenceCapsConfig struct {
42		MaxPromptBytes       *int                             `yaml:"max_prompt_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_prompt_bytes,omitempty"`
43		MaxInlinedFileBytes  *int                             `yaml:"max_inlined_file_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_inlined_file_bytes,omitempty"`
44		MaxDiffBytes         *int                             `yaml:"max_diff_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_diff_bytes,omitempty"`
45		MaxGoverningDocBytes *int                             `yaml:"max_governing_doc_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_governing_doc_bytes,omitempty"`
46		PerHarness           map[string]*EvidenceCapsOverride `yaml:"per_harness,omitempty" json:"per_harness,omitempty"`
47		// ContextBudget is the prompt budget label that execute-bead consults
48		// when assembling the bead prompt. Empty string means "full budget";
49		// "minimal" omits large governing documents (cheap-tier path). See
50		// SD-024 §Stage 3 — ExecuteBeadOptions migration.
51		ContextBudget string `yaml:"context_budget,omitempty" json:"context_budget,omitempty"`
52	}
53	
54	// ResolveContextBudget returns the effective context-budget label for this
55	// config. Defaults to "" (full budget) when unset.
56	func (e *EvidenceCapsConfig) ResolveContextBudget() string {
57		if e == nil {
58			return ""
59		}
60		return e.ContextBudget
61	}
62	
63	// EvidenceCapsOverride is the per-harness override shape inside
64	// `evidence_caps.per_harness`.
65	type EvidenceCapsOverride struct {
66		MaxPromptBytes       *int `yaml:"max_prompt_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_prompt_bytes,omitempty"`
67		MaxInlinedFileBytes  *int `yaml:"max_inlined_file_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_inlined_file_bytes,omitempty"`
68		MaxDiffBytes         *int `yaml:"max_diff_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_diff_bytes,omitempty"`
69		MaxGoverningDocBytes *int `yaml:"max_governing_doc_bytes,omitempty" json:"max_governing_doc_bytes,omitempty"`
70	}

72	// WorkersConfig controls the Add/Remove-worker affordances on the workers
73	// overview. `default_spec` supplies sane defaults for one-click worker
74	// dispatch; `max_count` optionally caps concurrent drain workers per project.
75	type WorkersConfig struct {
76		DefaultSpec *WorkerDefaultSpec `yaml:"default_spec,omitempty" json:"default_spec,omitempty"`
77		MaxCount    *int               `yaml:"max_count,omitempty" json:"max_count,omitempty"`
78		// NoProgressCooldown bounds how long a bead may sit without a phase
79		// transition before the execute-loop forces the worker to give up. Parsed
80		// via time.ParseDuration (e.g. "6h"). Empty string uses the built-in
81		// default (6h). See SD-024 §Config extensions §WorkersConfig.
82		NoProgressCooldown string `yaml:"no_progress_cooldown,omitempty" json:"no_progress_cooldown,omitempty"`
83		// MaxNoChangesBeforeClose caps the number of consecutive no_changes
84		// attempts the execute-loop tolerates before closing the bead as
85		// no-progress. Zero or unset uses the built-in default (3). Negative
86		// values are treated as unset.
87		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose *int `yaml:"max_no_changes_before_close,omitempty" json:"max_no_changes_before_close,omitempty"`
88		// HeartbeatInterval is how often a claim owner refreshes its heartbeat
89		// while holding a bead. Parsed via time.ParseDuration (e.g. "15s"). Empty
90		// string uses the built-in default that matches bead.HeartbeatInterval
91		// (30s today).
92		HeartbeatInterval string `yaml:"heartbeat_interval,omitempty" json:"heartbeat_interval,omitempty"`
93	}

95	// Default values for WorkersConfig resolvers. These mirror the hardcoded
96	// constants the execute-loop uses today; bead 6/7 of SD-024 wires the
97	// resolvers into the loop.
98	const (
99		defaultNoProgressCooldown      = 6 * time.Hour
100		defaultMaxNoChangesBeforeClose = 3
101		defaultHeartbeatInterval       = 30 * time.Second
102	)
103	
104	// ResolveNoProgressCooldown returns the effective no-progress cooldown for
105	// this config. Defaults to 6h when unset or unparseable.
106	func (w *WorkersConfig) ResolveNoProgressCooldown() time.Duration {
107		if w == nil || w.NoProgressCooldown == "" {
108			return defaultNoProgressCooldown
109		}
110		d, err := time.ParseDuration(w.NoProgressCooldown)
111		if err != nil || d <= 0 {
112			return defaultNoProgressCooldown
113		}
114		return d
115	}
116	
117	// ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose returns the effective max no_changes cap.
118	// Defaults to 3 when unset or non-positive.
119	func (w *WorkersConfig) ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose() int {
120		if w == nil || w.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose == nil || *w.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose <= 0 {
121			return defaultMaxNoChangesBeforeClose
122		}
123		return *w.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
124	}
125	
126	// ResolveHeartbeatInterval returns the effective heartbeat refresh interval.
127	// Defaults to 30s (matching bead.HeartbeatInterval today) when unset or
128	// unparseable.
129	func (w *WorkersConfig) ResolveHeartbeatInterval() time.Duration {
130		if w == nil || w.HeartbeatInterval == "" {
131			return defaultHeartbeatInterval
132		}
133		d, err := time.ParseDuration(w.HeartbeatInterval)
134		if err != nil || d <= 0 {
135			return defaultHeartbeatInterval
136		}
137		return d
138	}

212		Routing         *RoutingConfig      `yaml:"routing,omitempty" json:"routing,omitempty"`

214		Triage          *TriageConfig       `yaml:"triage,omitempty" json:"triage,omitempty"`
215	}
216	
217	// TriageConfig controls the pre-Claim complexity gate (ddx-5bf4ee7e).
218	type TriageConfig struct {
219		// MaxDecompositionDepth caps recursive bead splitting. Children re-enter
220		// the gate; at cap the parent is blocked with label=needs-human-decomposition.
221		// Zero or unset uses the binary default (3). Configurable as
222		// agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth in .ddx/config.yaml.
223		MaxDecompositionDepth *int `yaml:"max_decomposition_depth,omitempty" json:"max_decomposition_depth,omitempty"`
224	}
225	
226	// ResolveMaxDecompositionDepth returns the effective depth cap for the triage
227	// gate. Defaults to 3 when unset or non-positive.
228	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveMaxDecompositionDepth() int {
229		const defaultDepth = 3
230		if c == nil || c.Agent == nil || c.Agent.Triage == nil || c.Agent.Triage.MaxDecompositionDepth == nil {
231			return defaultDepth
232		}
233		if *c.Agent.Triage.MaxDecompositionDepth <= 0 {
234			return defaultDepth
235		}
236		return *c.Agent.Triage.MaxDecompositionDepth
237	}
238	
239	// AgentEndpoint describes one endpoint-first native agent provider target.
240	// Name and model are intentionally absent: routing discovers the live model IDs
241	// from the endpoint's /v1/models response at dispatch time.
242	type AgentEndpoint struct {
243		Type    string `yaml:"type,omitempty" json:"type,omitempty"`
244		Host    string `yaml:"host,omitempty" json:"host,omitempty"`
245		Port    int    `yaml:"port,omitempty" json:"port,omitempty"`
246		BaseURL string `yaml:"base_url,omitempty" json:"base_url,omitempty"`
247		APIKey  string `yaml:"api_key,omitempty" json:"api_key,omitempty"`
248		// RequestTimeoutSeconds is the wall-clock cap for a single Chat/ChatStream
249		// call to this endpoint. Zero means "use the model-class default" (15 min
250		// for standard models, 60 min for known thinking/reasoning models).
251		// Increase this for local thinking models that need >15 min to produce
252		// their first body delta (e.g. qwen3.6-35b on lmstudio).
253		// Example: request_timeout_seconds: 3600  # 1 hour
254		RequestTimeoutSeconds int `yaml:"request_timeout_seconds,omitempty" json:"request_timeout_seconds,omitempty"`
255	}
256	
257	// RoutingConfig is the agent routing policy block.
258	//
259	// Note: agent.routing.default_harness was REMOVED in the routing-config
260	// deprecation pass (bead ddx-87fb72c2). agent.routing.profile_ladders and
261	// agent.routing.model_overrides were removed in bead ddx-3bd7396a. Configs
262	// that still carry any of these fields fail to load with a migration message.
263	type RoutingConfig struct {
264		// ProfilePriority is the deprecated flat-list form; still parsed so
265		// existing configs can emit a deprecation warning.
266		ProfilePriority []string `yaml:"profile_priority,omitempty" json:"profile_priority,omitempty"`
267	}
268	
269	// AgentRunnerConfig was the embedded DDx Agent harness config block.
270	// Deprecated: Use native .agent/config.yaml instead. This type is retained for
271	// schema compatibility so existing configs with agent_runner blocks parse without error,
272	// but DDx no longer reads or applies these values.
273	type AgentRunnerConfig struct {
274		Provider      string `yaml:"provider,omitempty" json:"provider,omitempty"`
275		BaseURL       string `yaml:"base_url,omitempty" json:"base_url,omitempty"`
276		APIKey        string `yaml:"api_key,omitempty" json:"api_key,omitempty"`
277		Model         string `yaml:"model,omitempty" json:"model,omitempty"`
278		Preset        string `yaml:"preset,omitempty" json:"preset,omitempty"`
279		MaxIterations int    `yaml:"max_iterations,omitempty" json:"max_iterations,omitempty"`
280	}
281	
282	// LLMPresetConfig defines a named LLM configuration with optional multi-endpoint support.
283	// Deprecated: kept for schema compatibility; no longer read by DDx code.
284	type LLMPresetConfig struct {
285		Model     string   `yaml:"model" json:"model"`
286		Provider  string   `yaml:"provider,omitempty" json:"provider,omitempty"`
287		Endpoints []string `yaml:"endpoints,omitempty" json:"endpoints,omitempty"`
288		APIKey    string   `yaml:"api_key,omitempty" json:"api_key,omitempty"`
289		Strategy  string   `yaml:"strategy,omitempty" json:"strategy,omitempty"`
290	}
291	
292	// VirtualConfig configures the virtual agent harness.
293	type VirtualConfig struct {
294		Normalize []NormalizePattern `yaml:"normalize,omitempty" json:"normalize,omitempty"`
295	}
296	
297	// NormalizePattern is a regex→replacement pair applied to prompts before hashing.
298	type NormalizePattern struct {
299		Pattern string `yaml:"pattern" json:"pattern"`
300		Replace string `yaml:"replace" json:"replace"`
301	}
302	
303	// SystemConfig represents system-level configuration settings
304	type SystemConfig struct {
305		MetaPrompt *string `yaml:"meta_prompt,omitempty" json:"meta_prompt,omitempty"`
306	}
307	
308	// LibraryConfig represents library configuration settings
309	type LibraryConfig struct {
310		Path       string            `yaml:"path,omitempty" json:"path,omitempty"`
311		Repository *RepositoryConfig `yaml:"repository" json:"repository"`
312	}
313	
314	// BeadConfig represents bead tracker configuration settings.
315	type BeadConfig struct {
316		IDPrefix string `yaml:"id_prefix,omitempty" json:"id_prefix,omitempty"`
317	}
318	
319	// RepositoryConfig represents repository settings for the new format
320	type RepositoryConfig struct {
321		URL    string `yaml:"url" json:"url"`
322		Branch string `yaml:"branch" json:"branch"`
323	}
324	
325	// UpdateCheckConfig represents update checking settings
326	type UpdateCheckConfig struct {
327		Enabled   bool   `yaml:"enabled"`
328		Frequency string `yaml:"frequency"` // Duration: "24h", "12h", etc.
329	}
330	
331	// DefaultNewConfig returns a new config with default values applied
332	func DefaultNewConfig() *NewConfig {
333		return &NewConfig{
334			Version: "1.0",
335			Library: &LibraryConfig{
336				Path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx",
337				Repository: &RepositoryConfig{
338					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
339					Branch: "main",
340				},
341			},
342			PersonaBindings: make(map[string]string),
343			UpdateCheck: &UpdateCheckConfig{
344				Enabled:   true,
345				Frequency: "24h",
346			},
347		}
348	}
349	
350	// DefaultConfig is an alias for DefaultNewConfig for compatibility
351	var DefaultConfig = DefaultNewConfig()
352	
353	// GetMetaPrompt returns the meta-prompt path, defaulting to focused.md if unset
354	// Returns empty string if explicitly set to null/empty (disabled)
355	func (c *NewConfig) GetMetaPrompt() string {
356		if c.System == nil || c.System.MetaPrompt == nil {
357			// Unset: return default
358			return "claude/system-prompts/focused.md"
359		}
360		// Explicitly set (could be empty string to disable)
361		return *c.System.MetaPrompt
362	}
363	
364	// ApplyDefaults ensures all required fields have default values
365	func (c *NewConfig) ApplyDefaults() {
366		if c.Version == "" {
367			c.Version = "1.0"
368		}
369		if c.Library == nil {
370			c.Library = &LibraryConfig{
371				Path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx",
372				Repository: &RepositoryConfig{
373					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
374					Branch: "main",
375				},
376			}
377		} else {
378			if c.Library.Path == "" {
379				c.Library.Path = ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
380			}
381			if c.Library.Repository == nil {
382				c.Library.Repository = &RepositoryConfig{
383					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
384					Branch: "main",
385				}
386			} else {
387				if c.Library.Repository.URL == "" {
388					c.Library.Repository.URL = "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library"
389				}
390				if c.Library.Repository.Branch == "" {
391					c.Library.Repository.Branch = "main"
392				}
393			}
394		}
395		if c.Bead == nil {
396			c.Bead = &BeadConfig{}
397		}
398		if c.PersonaBindings == nil {
399			c.PersonaBindings = make(map[string]string)
400		}
401		if c.UpdateCheck == nil {
402			c.UpdateCheck = &UpdateCheckConfig{
403				Enabled:   true,
404				Frequency: "24h",
405			}
406		} else {
407			if c.UpdateCheck.Frequency == "" {
408				c.UpdateCheck.Frequency = "24h"
409			}
410		}
411	}

func TestSchemaAcceptsTopLevelTriageBlock(t *testing.T) {
	v, err := NewValidator()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	yamlDoc := []byte(`version: "1.0"
triage:
  policies:
    review_block: [re_attempt_with_context, escalate_tier, needs_human]
`)
	require.NoError(t, v.Validate(yamlDoc))
}

func TestSchemaRejectsUnknownTriageActionAndMode(t *testing.T) {
	v, err := NewValidator()
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Error(t, v.Validate([]byte(`version: "1.0"
triage:
  policies:
    review_block: [bogus_action]
`)))
	require.Error(t, v.Validate([]byte(`version: "1.0"
triage:
  policies:
    bogus_mode: [needs_human]
`)))
}
